Classify the political lean of this article:

---
HEADLINE: Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores 02:12 ГўВҖВў Source: CNN Classes o
SOURCE: CNN (Opinion)

ARTICLE:

Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores 02:12 ГўВҖВў Source: CNN Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores 02:12 CNN вҖ” Students who struggled through the height of the Covid-19 pandemic ГўВҖВ“ between March 2020 and early 2023 ГўВҖВ“ are still having trouble keeping up with course work, according to a study from the nonprofit NWEA, which focuses on education research. ГўВҖВңThis analysis provides the most current evidence to help guide recovery efforts and resource allocations in support of schools,ГўВҖВқ an NWEA news release said. ГўВҖВңWhile the pandemic is now deemed over, the impacts on students based on two markers, achievement in reading and mathematics, are still apparent.ГўВҖВқ Sixth-grade students in Topeka, Kansas, set up their lockers on the first day of classes in August 2022. Evert Nelson/The Capital-Journal/USA Today Network Related article Test scores for 13-year-olds drop several points since the start of pandemic, building on decade-long decline, report says The study used data from 6.7 million public school students from grades 3-8, the release said. According to the study, ГўВҖВңIn nearly all grades, achievement gains during 2022-23 fell short of prepandemic trends, which stalled progress toward pandemic recovery.ГўВҖВқ The performance gap widened in some grades when comparing students affected by Covid-19 and those before the pandemic, the data showed. The study found that students need an additional 4.1 months to catch up in reading and 4.5 months of additional instruction to catch up in math. Among those students, Black and Hispanic students ГўВҖВңremain furthest from recovery,ГўВҖВқ the release said. ГўВҖВңCOVID-19 may no longer be an emergency, but we are very much still dealing with the fallout from the crisis,ГўВҖВқ said Dr. Karyn Lewis, co-author of the study and director of the Center for School and Student Progress at NWEA, according to the release. Some students in the US are struggling with math and reading after the Covid-19 pandemic, a new study shows. Jose Luis Pelaez Inc/Digital Vision/Getty Images ГўВҖВңThese data reiterate that recovery will not be linear, easy, or quick and we cannot take our foot off the gas pedal,ГўВҖВқ Lewis said. ГўВҖВңDisappointing as these results may be, itГўВҖВҷs important not to lose sight of the fact that things would likely be so much worse without the enduring work of educators and schools to support students in this moment.ГўВҖВқ The NWEAГўВҖВҷs recommendations include using local data to help students continue to recover, intervening as needed and communicating important information with families in a timely way. The NWEA describes itself as a ГўВҖВңpioneer in educational research and assessment methodology,ГўВҖВқ with over four decades of work.
---
